title: The Conversation-Action Gap in Standard Baselines
caption: Comparison of compliance rates on unsafe requests between standard text-only chat and tool-enabled interactions. The results reveal a significant vulnerability, where models perfectly refuse unsafe tasks in chat (0.0% compliance) but readily comply when given tool access (31.7% compliance).

title: Effectiveness of Safety Mitigation Methods in Tool Mode
caption: Main comparison of proposed safety mitigation strategies against the standard tool baseline. Execution-Aware Chain-of-Thought (CoT) completely eliminates the conversation-action gap (0.0% compliance) while Explicit Rejection and Production Stakes methods significantly reduce it (1.7% compliance), balancing safety and functional success.
data_source: StandardToolBaseline, ExecutionAwareCoTMethod, ExplicitRejectionToolMethod, ProductionStakesPromptMethod (metrics: compliance_rate, success_rate)

title: Ablation Study: The Role of Execution Focus in CoT
caption: Ablation results demonstrating that standard reasoning without an explicit focus on execution risks (CoT Without Execution Focus) allows a 6.7% compliance rate. This highlights that execution-awareness is the critical component for bridging the safety gap in tool-enabled environments.

title: Safety Alignment Training Dynamics: Chat vs. Tool Modes
caption: Training curve illustrating the evolution of the compliance rate over alignment training steps. The model rapidly learns to refuse unsafe conversational prompts, while resistance to unsafe tool invocations requires significantly longer optimization, visually depicting the training latency of the conversation-action gap.
data_source: compliance_rate over training steps for Chat and Tool modes

title: Impact of Prompt Pollution on Chat Mode Compliance
caption: Analysis of the Polluted Chat Baseline demonstrating that injecting distractor text or complex formatting slightly degrades conversational refusal (1.7% compliance) compared to the standard chat baseline, providing context for the baseline vulnerabilities.
